title: Understanding Why Healthcare Professionals Should Not Give Away Fillers As Prizes
filename: Understanding Why Healthcare Professionals Should Not Give Away Fillers As Prizes.en.vtt
excerpt: Good evening everyone it's an amazing summer evening I thought I'd do a little live from outside I think I wanted to be outside really I thought I would talk about something that's come up on a forum today which is about prizes treatments as prizes which is if you're not if you'r…

segments.text: good evening everyone it's an amazing summer evening I thought I'd do a little live from outside I think I wanted to be outside really I thought I would talk about something that's come up on a forum today which is about prizes treatments as prizes which is if you're not if you're new to the industry and you've basically been learning about the industry of Eire Instagram and Facebook I think new even clinicians will come in and think great that's how you market your business you give away treatments as prizes so you know really understandable none of us like it's not often a detailed part of your training even if it's mentioned so people basically copy other people and they start giving away treatments as prizes until well quite often happens is another criticize them or you know there's a lot of that kind of attacking

type thing and so first thing I'd say is it's best to be kind first like assume that someone just doesn't has copied someone else and has got it wrong and if you tell them the right way to do things that they will be better but what I really wanted to cover is why like because I don't think a lot of people fully understand why we shouldn't give treatments away as prizes and why basically health care professionals are held at a much higher standard than other businesses in terms of how we can market ourselves so it all boils down to the same reason why we can't advertise Botox and it's about patient autonomy so if you are if you give away a prize as a treatment and you know something has a value of two or three hundred pounds and it's very easy on Facebook just to share something at Target and then you think well maybe I'll be in a chance you know in France for winning their prize the fact that you've given that prize then basically erodes the patient's autonomy so it effectively

puts an external force on their decision-making process so that they may have that procedure for the wrong and if you're a healthcare professional you are all about patient's well-being you can't be about leveraging you know prizes to build your business ahead of your patients well-being so this is kind of um it's often overlooked and I think actually we should be very proud that we don't give procedures away as prizes and you don't you know prescription drugs are waste prizes because that's what sets us apart because we want our patients to make a decision that is in their best interest that isn't eroded that isn't kind of leveraged over them a fear of missing out because if you win a prize you kind of feel like I don't want to miss out on that I want to make the most of it because it's worth a lot of money so that's why we don't give prizes away it's also why we don't advertise because if you advertise you're also kind of selling the benefits of a brisk Reacher of a procedure rather than helping your patient make the right

decision that will improve their well-being which is what we should be doing so good point there from I don't think is it be about it's a little bit gray because you need to have a non prescription non procedure thing that they could use those vouchers on but that is a way of preserving autonomy also vouchers can be more easily given away or even possibly sold but so I suppose that could be true of treatments too but it's does you just want to keep it really simple that there's something that you're prize that you can have that has zero risk and that's the other point I want to make the whole thing with procedures and prescriptions is that there is risk involved so you you're giving away something that has risk that's like you know and either you take this risk or you lose out that's kind of how it's seen by the GMC so we don't want to be putting patients in a position where they either have to take a risk with their health which is effectively what a prescription

drug or procedure is or they lose out on the prize so I think it's really important I think if you're already acknowledged you're already aware of this and you only give away gift vouchers that's great if you've made the mistake and you're giving away a prize because you've copied other people this you know now if you see other people doing it I suppose you could tag them in this video that would be cool and then they can be advised in quite a gentle way how to improve things allison asks another important question which is can you do offers it's all related to exactly the same topic which is du is the way that you present the offer going to put a an external force on their decision making process so that they do it for reasons other than their own so that's what time limited offers come in if you make the offer like book now you know you've got 24 hours to book that you've eroded their responsibility their autonomy and it's unethical so you can do an offer and we do offers occasionally but it's kind of just there's an offer on there's

no time limit so on and then it offers suddenly ends and they won't be - but there's never part of the offer that you need to book right away now so that's the kind of thing you can do so but it's but all of these things are linked to protecting patient autonomy they're not just random things to make healthcare professionals lives more difficult I do understand it really annoys us when we see non healthcare professionals doing it and doing it at scale that's infuriating because there is nothing you can do like and share stuff with a chance to win a voucher is absolutely fine because but you need to have something in your clinic that is non risky that they can spend the veterans I think I've said that already it's to protect patients so we should be proud of it and if you see a health care professional who is making that mistake I really believe in the best of people

we all none of us want to be that healthcare professional who's a rape who's eroding patients autonomy they just haven't thought about it deeply enough they're copying other people and you know we've all made mistakes when you think the way things work are one way and you just haven't been fully informed and of course lots of other people are break are using this method of marketing if you go on Instagram and say how do I grow my Instagram account people say hold the competition so what do people do they hold a competition but we're different because we're healthcare professionals and we're supposed to be protecting patients well-being so um yeah loads of thank you very much for watching if you thought this useful tag someone if you want to make the point to your patients you can share this but I hope it's helpful and thank you all very much for watching any questions please ask them down below and I'll try to answer them either in another video or I will comment down below but I hope you're enjoying the summer this crazy ridiculously good summer that we're having and yeah see you in the next video thanks to everyone
for watching thank you Miranda thanks for that comment
